graded_decisions.news_since: Worst monthly decline since 2008; $190B FY2026 capex fueling margin fears; Stifel downgrade to hold; quantum computing claims refuted by Nature journal (2026-06-24); Xbox hardware revenue -33% YoY (GuruFocus, Motley Fool, 2026-06-25)
graded_decisions.comment: Entry was above the entire subsequent 15-day price range. The AI-cloud thesis is under structural pressure as the market reprices capex risk — Microsoft is spending $190B in FY2026 with return timelines unclear. Multiple-compression is accelerating; no invalidation condition was logged at entry.

graded_decisions.news_since: B200 GPU spot price collapsed from $6.11 to $4.22/hr (-31%) May-June 2026; China AI chip revenue effectively zero (Jensen Huang confirmed); $410.6M insider selling in 3 months; semiconductor sector -6% on June 5 Broadcom AI outlook miss (GuruFocus, indmoney, Yahoo Finance, 2026-06)
graded_decisions.comment: GPU spot pricing is a real-time demand indicator that was in freefall at or before entry — a contra-signal that was publicly available. China market exit removes a significant TAM. Record Q1 FY2027 revenue ($81.6B, +85% YoY) shows long-term demand intact but near-term pricing power is eroding and insider selling at scale raises a red flag.

graded_decisions.news_since: Nasdaq -4% on June 5 semiconductor slide (TheStreet); -2.2% on June 23 tech sell-off; tech accelerated lower June 24-26 on AI valuation concerns and OpenAI IPO delay (CNBC, TheStreet, 2026-06-26)
graded_decisions.comment: Holding QQQ alongside direct MSFT and NVDA positions creates triple-counted Mega-cap tech exposure (MSFT and NVDA are top-5 QQQ holdings). This amplifies drawdowns without adding diversification. QQQ fell less than the single stocks but still significantly underperformed SPY.

graded_decisions.news_since: S&P 500 broadly resilient amid tech sell-off; non-tech sector strength absorbed Nasdaq weakness; SPY held near entry while tech fell 10-13% (CNBC, 2026-06)
graded_decisions.comment: Best-performing holding by a wide margin. Broad diversification provided meaningful cushion vs the concentrated tech positions. Broad market thesis remains intact.

patterns: 100% of long market value is in US tech and tech-adjacent names (MSFT, NVDA, QQQ, SPY — all highly correlated in a sector sell-off); portfolio fell -3.6% vs SPY -1.6% this week, a -2.0pp sector-concentration tax
Both single-stock entries (MSFT @$425.31, NVDA @$217.09) were made above the subsequent 15-day high — buying into strength immediately before sector de-rating, with no logged invalidation condition
QQQ held alongside MSFT and NVDA creates triple-counted Mega-cap tech exposure; this amplifies drawdowns without adding diversification
No decision journal entries exist for the review window — the daily routine made no logged decisions; there is no audit trail of thesis, entry rationale, or invalidation conditions
AI-theme entries missed contra-signals visible at or before entry: GPU spot prices in freefall (-31%), NVDA China revenue at zero, MSFT capex ballooning to $190B, Broadcom AI outlook miss June 4
suggested_adjustments: Log every trade immediately in decisions.jsonl with: symbol, action, thesis (<=2 sentences), explicit invalidation condition, price target, and >=2 named sources — no entry without a logged thesis

Before any new tech-sector long, check GPU spot pricing (e.g. Vast.ai B200 hourly rates) and any pending capex announcements; if spot pricing is declining week-over-week, require a rebuttal thesis from 2+ outlets before proceeding
Cap tech+tech-adjacent concentration at 50% of long market value; do not add a name already held in QQQ or SPY without a materially distinct single-stock thesis (avoids triple-counting)

Set an explicit invalidation price at entry — if a position falls >8% from entry with no thesis-confirming news in the interim, flag it in the next session for review rather than holding silently through -10% to -13% drawdowns
summary: First review (journal empty — no logged decisions to grade). All four positions are underwater since entry. MSFT (-13.3%) and NVDA (-11.0%) bore the brunt of June's tech rout — worst for MSFT since 2008 — driven by AI capex concerns, GPU spot-price collapse (-31%), semiconductor de-rating, and OpenAI IPO delay. QQQ (-4.3%) fell less but amplified the tech pain given overlapping holdings. SPY (-0.1% since entry) was the only bright spot. Portfolio fell -3.6% this week vs SPY -1.6%, VT -2.0%, SSO -3.2%; all passive benchmarks beat the portfolio, with SPY the best. Alpha vs SPY: -2.0pp. Note: SSO's -3.2% is worse than 2x SPY's -1.6% due to negative daily-reset compounding in a declining market. Critical structural gaps: no decision logging, extreme tech concentration, and triple-counted Mega-cap exposure via MSFT+NVDA+QQQ. Immediate priorities: implement mandatory thesis logging, set invalidation stops at -8%, and diversify beyond US tech.
